summary: Case presents compulsive door-checking with certainty-driven memory doubt (C01), repetitive checking (C02), difficulty inhibiting checking (C03), time cost/late departures (C04), social embarrassment (C05), and self-criticisms after episodes (C06). The following candidate, modifiable BIO-PSYCHO-SOCIAL levers span cognitive-behavioral techniques, environmental design, implementation strategies, self-compassion, mindfulness, social support, and sleep/circadian optimization. Each lever targets one or more of the specified criteria, includes a concrete measurement plan, a reasonable time horizon, and feasibility/impact estimates to guide prioritization. Safety notes accompany any intervention that could affect distress or safety.

solutions.solution_candidate: Brief ERP module: graded exposure to leaving the door with no checking, plus a structured response-prevention rule (e.g., commit to leaving after initial lock without re-check). Use a daily checklist to track episodes and urge strength.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Exposure with response prevention weakens the conditioned urge to re-check by habituation and inhibitory learning, reducing frequency of checks (C02), the urge to check (C03), and lateness due to checks (C04).
solutions.measurement: Weekly count of checks per leaving episode; average departure delay in minutes; self-reported urge intensity after leaving (0–10).
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor distress; escalate to clinician if compulsions intensify or impairment worsens.
solutions.mapping_query: S01 maps to C02, C03, C04

solutions.solution_candidate: Micro-metacognitive training to foster acceptance of memory for locking; use reality-testing prompts and a brief memory-confidence diary to reduce doubt about locking (C01).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Metacognitive strategies reduce the need for constant certainty about memory, promoting acceptance of the locking action as sufficient.
solutions.measurement: Post-locking memory acceptance rating (0–10); daily doubts count.
solutions.safety_notes: Avoid framing that could pathologize normal doubt; emphasize supportive framing.

solutions.solution_candidate: Install lock-status visual indicator and a mandated brief delay before re-check (e.g., one-minute delay). Optional smartphone notification of lock state to provide external feedback and reduce reliance on internal memory; minimize re-check triggers.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: External feedback reduces dependence on internal certainty signals and creates a safe delay that decreases repeated checking and lateness.
solutions.measurement: Frequency of post-initial-lock checks per leaving episode; departure timeliness (on-time vs late).
solutions.safety_notes: Privacy considerations for notifications; adjust if alerts increase anxiety.

solutions.solution_candidate: Create a precommitment: after locking, enforce a fixed short delay (e.g., 2 minutes) before leaving, supported by an alarm or calendar reminder. Use an leaving-ritual to standardize the departure process and reduce opportunistic checks.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Implementation intentions create automatic cues that reduce reliance on momentary doubt and decrease late departures and checks.
solutions.measurement: On-time departure rate; checks per leaving episode; time from initial lock to exit.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure the delay fits daily schedule; adjust as needed to avoid undue stress.

solutions.solution_candidate: Brief self-compassion exercises post-episode; rewrite self-talk; gratitude/forgiveness journaling to mitigate post-check self-criticism (C06).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reducing harsh self-criticism after lapses decreases maladaptive affect that can reinforce future checking and rumination.
solutions.measurement: Post-episode self-criticism rating (0–10); frequency of negative self-talk entries.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for overgeneralization of self-worth; emphasize compassionate framing.

solutions.solution_candidate: Guided mindfulness/acceptance practices focusing on uncertainty and the urge to re-check (urge surfing) after locking.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Acceptance-based strategies reduce impulsive checking by attenuating craving-like urges and improving inhibitory control.
solutions.measurement: Urge intensity after leaving (0–10); number of checks; time to depart.
solutions.safety_notes: Provide clear guidance to avoid avoidance; ensure proper practice.

solutions.solution_candidate: Engage a trusted buddy to support leaving routines; share plan; use scheduled check-ins to reduce embarrassment and improve punctuality (C04, C05).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social accountability reduces self-consciousness-related delays and supports consistent leaving behavior.
solutions.measurement: On-time departure rate; embarrassment rating; partner interaction frequency.
solutions.safety_notes: Respect autonomy; ensure partner is supportive and voluntary.

solutions.solution_candidate: Sleep hygiene and circadian optimization: regular sleep schedule, daylight exposure, limit caffeine late; aim for 7–9 hours to support executive function and memory reliability (C01, C03, C04).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved sleep enhances memory consolidation and executive control, reducing doubt and improving timely departure.
solutions.measurement: Average sleep duration; sleep quality score; days with no checks; departure times.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for caffeine-related anxiety; adjust as needed.
solutions.mapping_query: S08 maps to C01, C03, C04
